channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: 'A single cyber incident can lead to physical disruption, create safety hazards, or cause catastrophic downtime': Hackers target data center equipment, including critical power devices, in latest push to disrupt communities
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers found vulnerabilities in power and cooling systems inside data centers that could allow attackers to disrupt physical infrastructure operations

item.title: I rounded up the best Toy Story 5 toys and merch — these are the high-tech figures, Lego sets, and apparel worth buying
item.description: Toy Story 5 hits theaters June 19, and we've found the best toys, merch, and collectibles to shop before you see it

item.title: ChatGPT now has a hub for scheduled tasks
item.description: TIL you can schedule prompts in ChatGPT.

item.title: VSCO launches Studio Pro mobile photo editing app and plans $500 per year subscription
item.description: VSCO is taking on Adobe with a new Studio Pro editing app rolling out today on iOS and coming to macOS later this year, as Bloomberg reports. At launch, the app offers tools for batch editing, style matching from a reference image, and sharing images through VSCO Galleries. VSCO says more features are coming later, […]

item.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
item.description: The former Sequoia Capital leader is filling an "existing vacancy" on SpaceX's board, days after the company went public in the largest IPO ever.

item.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
item.description: Snap's long-awaited smart glasses debut hasn't exactly done wonders for the company's stock.

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
item.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses for parts of their org, and Meta killed its internal leaderboard. This tension between […]...

item.title: Epic Games details how it's embracing generative AI in Unreal Engine
item.description: Epic Games is making generative AI a big part of upcoming versions of Unreal Engine.

item.title: How to watch Ghana vs Panama: Free Streams & TV Channels for FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Here's how to watch Ghana vs Panama for free online and from anywhere in a crucial Group L clash at the FIFA World Cup 2026, with knockout hopes on the line.

item.title: FTC lawsuit reveals how subscription scam networks evade app store enforcement
item.description: A new FTC lawsuit reveals how sophisticated subscription app operators can allegedly use shell companies and payment infrastructure to stay active on app stores despite mounting consumer complaints.

item.title: I wish more wearables were as repairable as Google’s Pixel Watch 4
item.description: Google’s Pixel Watch 4 highlights the benefits of repairable wearables and why the industry should embrace easier device repairs.

item.title: Samsung veut diagnostiquer la santé de votre chien avec une simple photo Galaxy
item.description: Samsung veut transformer votre Galaxy en premier filtre de diagnostic pour chiens et chats : une photo, et l’IA cherche des signes de maladie. Pratique, mais à prendre avec des pincettes.

item.title: Nintendo responds after alleged third-party data breach: Our ‘systems have not been compromised’
item.description: Nintendo has reportedly been threatened with a $2 million ransomware demand. The company says its systems are secure.

item.title: Anthropic’s updated Claude Design gives vibe coders—and their design overlords—more control
item.description: Anthropic heard your feedback. A new version of Claude Design is better with design systems, has finer-tuned editing controls, and is more efficient with tokens. Claude Design, Anthropic Lab’s AI design tool, launched in April to a crowded market of vibe-coding design programs. Now, after a few months in the hands of designers, Anthropic is launching a big update to Claude Design that addresses their biggest pain points....

item.title: Easing housing market lock-in? 47% of homeowners say they’d accept up to 6.0% mortgage rate on their next purchase
item.description: Here are the full results of the Q2 2026 TurboHome-ResiClub Housing Sentiment Survey. Want more housing market stories from Lance Lambert’s ResiClub in your inbox? Subscribe to the ResiClub newsletter.

item.title: The AI credibility gap is real
item.description: Here’s what actually closes it. Lately, I’ve seen a specific pattern emerge as organizations make AI claims. “We’re AI-first.” “We’re AI-native.” “We’re agentic.” The language is confident, forward-looking, and nearly universal.
